title: Add pictures to monday forms
excerpt: I would love to add images to my Monday forms. We regularly send out surveys where clients specify their design preferences, and having the option to include images in the questions would greatly enhance clarity and user…

posts.author: leahlering (Lea)
posts.cookedHtml: I would love to add images to my Monday forms. We regularly send out surveys where clients specify their design preferences, and having the option to include images in the questions would greatly enhance clarity and user experience. This feature would make it much easier for clients to provide accurate feedback. Thank you!

posts.author: Nachliel (Nachliel from Easyapps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @leahlering

Nachliel from Easyapps here, our monday form builder Easyform supports CSS styles in the description of the form, as well as the form questions, meaning you can control the size and alignment of the images you embed in the form.

For example, here I am embedding the same image twice, in Text 1 I’m using the plain
<img src="https://i.postimg.cc/657fn9Tt/Profile-3.png"/>

while in Text 2 I’m adding styles
<img src="https://i.postimg.cc/657fn9Tt/Profile-3.png" style="display: block; margin: 0 auto; width: 100px" />

eazyform.app_form_mfs-2613JbQCafKduxoB1732×1464 144 KB

For any questions contact us at easyformsupport@easyappshq.com.

Check out our extensive documentation site.

You can book a free Zoom call with us and we will help you set up the form.

posts.author: DavidSchenkler (David Schenkler | monday ambassador cohort 7)
posts.cookedHtml:
Read my post here to learn how to do this!

Embedding images to Workform descriptions Special workflows & use cases
Ever wanted to embed an image directly into your question description? Here’s how you can do it! Step 1: Host your image First, upload your image to Postimages (or another image-hosting site). Choose any expiration time you want Select your preferred image size After uploading, copy the Direct Link (not the thumbnail or page link) Step 2: Add the HTML to your question In your question description, insert the following HTML: <img src="PASTE_DIRECT_LINK_HERE"> …

posts.author: kevin_vaghasiya (kevin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey, we do design surveys for clients too and ran into the same thing. Ended up going with FormFlow from the marketplace, it has rich text blocks where you can embed images directly into the form between questions. Makes a huge difference when clients need visual context.

Plus the multi-step layout keeps things clean instead of one long scrolling page.
